---
title: "Projects and Technical Portfolio - [Portfolio building is work in progress]"
description: "Selected analytical work, RPubs publications, and future case-study space."
---

<!-- This page turns the existing RPubs presence into a clearer portfolio. RPubs are kept as the archive while this site is used as the curated front door. -->

## Learning - Case Studies

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .project-card}
### Evidence Beyond Digitization: Lessons from Scaling HIV Information Systems in Rural Kenya 

During my work supporting a USG PEPFAR/CDC-funded HIV program in Kenya, I critically assessed evidence surrounding the digitization of HIV service delivery and reporting systems intended to improve data quality, program monitoring, and decision-making. The initiative transitioned more than 180 health facilities in 2 low resourced rural Counties from fragmented paper-based systems to a DHIS2-based platform and centralized data reporting and warehousing system.
Initial evidence suggested digitization would improve reporting accuracy, timeliness, and overall program performance. However, I identified important limitations in the evidence base. Much of the literature demonstrated improvements in reporting completeness and data availability, but there was limited rigorous evidence directly linking digitization to sustained patient outcomes such as retention in care or viral suppression. In addition, many successful digital health implementations described in the literature were conducted in settings with stronger infrastructure and technical capacity than the rural facilities where we operated.
During implementation, we encountered several challenges that directly affected the feasibility of scaling the system, including inconsistent historical paper records, low digital literacy among some health workers, unreliable connectivity, and weak facility-level data governance. Several remote facilities lacked reliable electricity, requiring investment in solar systems to support paperless operations. As a result, we adopted a phased implementation approach that classified facilities according to workload and infrastructure readiness, strengthened data quality audits, and introduced context-appropriate innovations such as SMS-based reporting into DHIS2 platforms.
The experience showed me that digital transformation alone is insufficient for health systems improvement; sustainable impact depends equally on infrastructure, governance, workforce capacity, and implementation approaches that fit local realities.
:::
::: 

## RPubs portfolio

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .project-card}
### Hierarchical and Mixed Effects Models in R
Technical note on multilevel and mixed effects modelling in R.

[View on RPubs](https://rpubs.com/Onduma/mixedeffects)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .project-card}
### Experimental Design in R
Applied notes on experimental design workflows in R.

[View on RPubs](https://rpubs.com/Onduma/experimental_design)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .project-card}
### Analyzing Survey Data in R
Applied survey data analysis notes and examples.

[View on RPubs](https://rpubs.com/Onduma/surveydata)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .project-card}
### Foundations of Inference
Personal notes from statistical inference learning material.

[View on RPubs](https://rpubs.com/Onduma/stat_inference)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .project-card}
### Modeling with tidymodels
Machine learning and modelling workflows using the tidymodels ecosystem.

[View on RPubs](https://rpubs.com/Onduma/tidymodels)
:::
:::

<!-- ## Recommended next project pages -->

<!-- This is work in progress -->

<!-- I will create one page per strong case study under `projects/`, using the following structure: -->

<!-- ```markdown -->
<!-- --- -->
<!-- title: "Project title" -->
<!-- description: "One-line summary" -->
<!-- --- -->

<!-- ## Problem -->
<!-- What decision or program question did this work address? -->

<!-- ## Data and methods -->
<!-- What data, tools, and analytical methods were used? -->

<!-- ## Results -->
<!-- What changed or what was learned? -->

<!-- ## Reproducibility -->
<!-- Link to code, RPubs, GitHub, dashboard, or report. -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- Good first candidates will include: -->

<!-- 1. NTD MEL systems and indicator frameworks -->
<!-- 2. Visceral leishmaniasis analytics and sustainability evidence -->
<!-- 3. Survey data analysis in R -->
<!-- 4. Mixed effects models for program or health data -->
<!-- 5. DHIS2 / EMR data quality improvement case study -->
